_meta.description: Per-image rationale for the bad-MUAC photo corpus. Mirror of reasons.json in the shared stock-images Drive folder (LABS_SYNTHETIC_STOCK_IMAGES_FOLDER_ID).

categories.tape_usage: MUAC tape is improperly applied — overflows the framing window, misaligned, or twisted.
categories.framing: Photo framing makes the reading impossible to verify — too zoomed, no arrows visible, or no arm context.
categories.equipment: The MUAC strip itself is damaged — faded arrows, worn edges, lost tick marks.
categories.misleading: Photo appears fraudulent — tape is on a finger, hand, or surface, not a child's arm.
categories.content_free: Image is unusable due to motion blur, focus, or obstruction; reading cannot be confirmed.

muac_bad_001.jpg.reason: MUAC tape overflows the framing reticle (not threaded back) — the loose end sticks out below the window.

muac_bad_002.jpg.reason: Tape is not threaded through the reticle at all; the strip lies flat across the arm so no measurement window is formed.

muac_bad_003.jpg.reason: Tape is twisted/rotated vertically so the reading window faces sideways and the numbers cannot be read in the normal orientation.

muac_bad_004.jpg.reason: Extreme zoom — only the digit '15' is visible, with no arrows, no arm, and no color zone for context.

muac_bad_005.jpg.reason: Framing is too distant and the reading window is small in the frame; the numbers around the arrows are difficult to read.

muac_bad_006.jpg.reason: Tape edges are worn — tick marks near 16-17-18 cm are scuffed off, reducing precision of the measurement.

muac_bad_007.jpg.reason: The red color zone on the strip is heavily scratched/peeling, which would compromise an FLW's ability to triage severe-acute cases by color.

muac_bad_008.jpg.reason: Tape is uniformly faded — arrows, tick marks, and numbers are nearly invisible and the reading cannot be confirmed.

muac_bad_009.jpg.reason: Tape is wrapped around the FLW's finger, not a child's arm — circumference is impossible at this diameter and the photo is fraudulent.

muac_bad_010.jpg.reason: Tape is laid flat against the FLW's thumb and palm rather than wrapped around a child's arm.

muac_bad_011.jpg.reason: Tape is wrapped around an adult finger — the diameter is far too small to be a child's mid-upper arm.

muac_bad_012.jpg.reason: The MUAC window is visible but the numeric reading is partially occluded and shot at a steep angle that distorts the value.

muac_bad_013.jpg.reason: Image is heavily out of focus — the reading window is a green blur and no digits are legible.
